analysis.problem: Inequities in access to quality education and literacy rates persist globally, hindering human development and dignity
analysis.root_cause: Systemic issues such as inadequate funding, lack of infrastructure, and societal inequalities contribute to the persistence of educational disparities
leverage_points: Global and national education policies and funding mechanisms
Community and family support systems for education

proposed_actions.action: Implement targeted educational programs and resources to address literacy gaps and improve access to quality education
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase global literacy rates by 10% within the next 5 years, as measured by UNESCO and World Bank data

evidence: The world has made progress in increasing basic education, but faces challenges in improving access and quality, as reported by Our World in Data
Literacy rates vary significantly across countries, with some having higher literacy rates than others, as shown in the Top 10 Countries with the Highest Literacy Rates in 2025 video
analysis.generalization: This problem is connected to the domains of PLANETA (through environmental factors influencing education), CHOVEK (through individual and community well-being), and CIVILIZATION (through economic and social structures affecting education)
analysis.summary: Educational inequities persist globally, but targeted programs and policies can help address literacy gaps and improve access to quality education, ultimately enhancing human development and dignity. Addressing these issues is crucial for achieving a sustainable civilization and promoting dignity for all.

raw_items.title: Global education
raw_items.summary: Global education is a mental development program that seeks to improve global human development based on the understanding of global dynamics, through the various sectors of human development delivery. raw_items.title: Global Education - Our World in Data
raw_items.summary: Explore data and insights on education outcomes, access, quality, and inequalities across the world. Learn how the world has made progress in increasing basic education, but also faces challenges in improving learning and reducing gaps.

raw_items.title: Literacy
raw_items.summary: Literacy is the ability to read and write, and illiteracy is the inability to read and write.
